meaning: n.(辭丞宛魍ｼ,閧臥ｭ臥噪)辭丞宛螳､
examples: Eels are cooked over a beech and oak fire at Dutchman Alex Koelewijn's smokehouse.
In neat little rows I tacked the hides on the smokehouse wall.
A ham hanging in the smokehouse signified insurance against hard times.
Luka, the town butcher, owned the pasture and the smokehouse on the edge of town.
He said the smokehouse gifts were sent to constituents probably including county party chairmen.
